title: oxalis corniculata var. radicosa
intro: Oxalis corniculata var. radicosa, also known as the creeping wood-sorrel, is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oxalis corniculata var. radicosa include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its use in managing diabetes and hypertension. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, decoctions, and infusions, and it can also be used as a tincture or added to topical creams and ointments for its medicinal properties.
